subject: [PATCH 0/4] nfsd: follow-on fixes for directory delegations

reviews.author: Jeff Layton
reviews.summary: Responded to Chuck's application of the patches by apologizing for a mistake in one of the patches and offering to resend it or have it fixed up.
reviews.raw_body:
On Sat, 2026-05-23 at 12:17 -0400, Jeff Layton wrote:
> inode->i_ino is now a u64. Use the same size for the tracepoint.
> 
> Signed-off-by: Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>
> ---
>  fs/nfsd/trace.h | 4 ++--
>  1 file changed, 2 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)
> 
> diff --git a/fs/nfsd/trace.h b/fs/nfsd/trace.h
> index 3d0f0bd30d90..1d5d11f914f2 100644
> --- a/fs/nfsd/trace.h
> +++ b/fs/nfsd/trace.h
> @@ -1384,7 +1384,7 @@ TRACE_EVENT(nfsd_handle_dir_event,
>  	TP_STRUCT__entry(
>  		__field(u32, mask)
>  		__field(dev_t, s_dev)
> -		__field(ino_t, i_ino)
> +		__field(ino_t, u64)

My apologies. This should read:

                __field(u64, i_ino)

I had the fix in my tree and forgot to commit it before sending. Would
you like me to resend this one, or do you just want to fix it up?

>  		__string_len(name, name ? name->name : NULL,
>  				   name ? name->len : 0)
>  	),
> @@ -1394,7 +1394,7 @@ TRACE_EVENT(nfsd_handle_dir_event,
>  		__entry->i_ino = dir ? dir->i_ino : 0;
>  		__assign_str(name);
>  	),
> -	TP_printk("inode=0x%x:0x%x:0x%lx mask=%s name=%s",
> +	TP_printk("inode=0x%x:0x%x:0x%llx mask=%s name=%s",
>  			MAJOR(__entry->s_dev), MINOR(__entry->s_dev),
>  			__entry->i_ino, show_fsnotify_mask(__entry->mask),
>  			__get_str(name))

-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: Fixed the issue in one of the patches and pushed out an update to nfsd-testing.
reviews.raw_body:
On Sun, May 24, 2026, at 8:09 AM, Jeff Layton wrote:
> On Sat, 2026-05-23 at 12:17 -0400, Jeff Layton wrote:
>> inode->i_ino is now a u64. Use the same size for the tracepoint.
>> 
>> Signed-off-by: Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>
>> ---
>>  fs/nfsd/trace.h | 4 ++--
>>  1 file changed, 2 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)
>> 
>> diff --git a/fs/nfsd/trace.h b/fs/nfsd/trace.h
>> index 3d0f0bd30d90..1d5d11f914f2 100644
>> --- a/fs/nfsd/trace.h
>> +++ b/fs/nfsd/trace.h
>> @@ -1384,7 +1384,7 @@ TRACE_EVENT(nfsd_handle_dir_event,
>>  	TP_STRUCT__entry(
>>  		__field(u32, mask)
>>  		__field(dev_t, s_dev)
>> -		__field(ino_t, i_ino)
>> +		__field(ino_t, u64)
>
> My apologies. This should read:
>
>                 __field(u64, i_ino)
>
> I had the fix in my tree and forgot to commit it before sending. Would
> you like me to resend this one, or do you just want to fix it up?
>
>>  		__string_len(name, name ? name->name : NULL,
>>  				   name ? name->len : 0)
>>  	),

I fixed this in place and pushed out the update to nfsd-testing.


-- 
Chuck Lever

2026-05-24.patch_summary: This patch series addresses several issues related to directory delegations in the NFS server (nfsd). The patches fix problems flagged during review, including checking for FILEID_INVALID in setup_notify_fhandle and using an empty string for the directory name in NOTIFY4_CHANGE_DIR_ATTRS. Additionally, the patches check delegation status in nfsd4_cb_notify_done and correct a format specifier issue in the nfsd_handle_dir_event tracepoint.
